methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Grain Weevil is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Ben Johnson that has raised $3.5M to date. The valuation exercise triangulates against a comp universe of 9 private robotics firms and 1 public comparable (277810.KQ), though peer-level valuation multiples proved insufficiently dense for reliable median benchmarking. Top private peers in the sector include 1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics.

With only one applicable valuation method, the analysis relies on stage-progression mechanics. Using the $3.5M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $8.75M. The peer-median method was not applied due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparable data points within the robotics sector narrowly filtered for valuation tier similarity.

The triangulated valuation range is $7M to $10M, with a median of $9M. This range reflects conservative downside ($7M, representing a 1.4x multiple on invested capital) and moderate upside ($10M). Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 2.5x multiplier to Grain Weevil's specific stage-gate dynamics and the limited applicability of the broader sector comp set. A peer-median approach would strengthen conviction once adjacent financing rounds populate comparable valuations in this subsector.
